recent_player_actions.text: Galinor approaches the tavern runner. "Runner, tell me something interesting."
"What? I asked you why people here wouldn't be friendly to newcomers."

frontier_gate.last_player_action_text: "What? I asked you why people here wouldn't be friendly to newcomers."

recent_contextual_leads.subject: rain spatters soot-dark stone while refugees, wagons, and foot traffic clog the muddy approach
guard captain holds the choke while the crowd presses in, and tavern runner shouts offers of hot stew and paid rumor
tavern runner starts to answer, then glances past you as shouting breaks out in the crowd

unknown_topic.last_answer: The Tavern Runner responds, "Because, believe me, there are people here who would like to keep things quiet. Not everyone welcomes an influx of new faces—especially those asking questions. You could find yourself in a bit of trouble if you aren't careful." Her eyes flicker briefly toward a group of rugged patrons watching you from the edge of the crowd.

topic:asked_friendly_newcomer.last_answer: The Tavern Runner furrows her brow, her voice low and cautious as she replies, "It's complicated. Some folks here believe newcomers often bring trouble, especially around these parts of the Old Crossroads. Best to tread carefully."  Her anxious demeanor suggests there's more to her words, possibly a deeper fear tied to the missing patrols or recent events.

rule_priority_order: DO NOT LEAK HIDDEN FACTS / SECRETS
IF FULL CERTAINTY IS UNAVAILABLE, GIVE A BOUNDED PARTIAL ANSWER
WHEN THE PLAYER PRESSES THE SAME TOPIC AGAIN, ADD NET-NEW VALUE RATHER THAN RESTATING

no_validator_voice.rule: Never speak as a validator, analyst, referee of canon, or system. Do not mention what is or is not established, available to the model, visible to tools, or answerable by the system. If uncertainty exists, express it as in-world uncertainty from people, circumstances, clues, distance, darkness, rumor, missing access, or incomplete observation.
